place: Kings Park New York, New York

facts.text: Kings Park began as a small agricultural settlement in the 1800s but grew rapidly after the opening of the Kings Park State Hospital in 1885.
The Kings Park Heritage Museum sits inside the historic train station that once served patients, staff, and visitors to the former psychiatric center.
The museum’s collection focuses on the Kings Park State Hospital and the hamlet’s broader history, illustrating how the hospital intertwined with but did not define the community.
Kings Park lies in the Town of Smithtown on Long Island’s scenic North Shore, celebrated for waterfront parks, historic charm, and a small‑town atmosphere.
The 2023 film “Leave the World Behind” shot scenes on Main Street in Kings Park, bringing a touch of Hollywood to the hamlet.
Kings Park is bordered by the Nissequogue River to the east, Fort Salonga to the west, Commack to the southwest, and Smithtown to the southeast.
